level_signals: Base salary range $180,000–$205,000 consistent with senior IC
Expected to own and define roadmap autonomously for a new, critical role
Requires proven track record of leading complex cross-functional initiatives

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Strong experience working with Salesforce as a product surface including objects, flows, integrations, and GTM processes and the ability to partner deeply with Salesforce admins and engineers
Proven track record of integrating multiple internal systems (such as CRM, billing engines, data warehouses, and internal tools) rather than optimizing a single product in isolation
Comfortable working with APIs, backend services, and data pipelines, and able to make informed tradeoffs across these layers including querying and interpreting data using SQL or similar
Experience leading complex, cross-functional initiatives that span Engineering, GTM, Finance, and Operations, with multiple dependencies and stakeholders
Ability to run agile product delivery end-to-end, from backlog management and writing epics to release planning, with distributed engineering teams
Global experience with the flexibility to work as needed across flexible timezones including Europe and South America, with some travel required (<25%)

scope.surface: US Go-to-Market platform: Salesforce, billing systems, partner residual tracking, internal tools, and GTM reporting — consolidating fragmented legacy systems into a unified global platform
scope.business_model: B2B fintech SaaS; serving small businesses via partner and direct sales channels
primary_metrics_inferred: Quote-to-cash flow reliability and data trust
Platform alignment with global SumUp platform
core_responsibilities: Own and prioritize the US platform roadmap across Salesforce, billing systems, partner residual tracking, internal tools, and key reporting surfaces
Map and continuously refine the end-to-end quote-to-cash and reporting flow
Work closely with Platform Engineering, Data, Finance, Revenue Operations, and GTM leadership to understand pain points and define requirements
Translate data needs to Engineering and Analytics teams to streamline and bubble up business and product requirements
Produce and maintain a clear, shared map of all relevant US systems and flows
Define and run the rituals that keep delivery on track: intake, backlog grooming, sprint ceremonies, and regular stakeholder updates
Identify and lead quick wins alongside longer-term integration work

green_flags: Generous PTO (22 days + increment up to 28, wellness days, sabbatical after 3 years)
JD acknowledges candidates need not meet 100% of criteria
New, critical role with broad ownership and genuine scope for impact
red_flags: No visa sponsorship available — hard blocker for non-authorized candidates
Office-first setup in Boulder, CO only — no remote option
No years-of-experience floor stated — seniority expectations may be ambiguous until screening
Role is brand new with no existing owner — may involve significant ambiguity and organizational complexity from the start
Some travel required and cross-timezone flexibility expected (Europe and South America)

The role is titled 'Technical Product Manager' but the responsibilities and cross-functional scope closely resemble a Technical Program Manager; archetype set to Technical Program Manager with secondary_archetype noting Platform PM flavor.
Nice-to-haves section is absent; all listed criteria appear in the 'You'll be great for this role if…' section and are treated as hard requirements.
